title: $display
text: $display Return the name of the current display device translation table. vDdt = $display Return Values Name of the device translation table being used for display. Table 1. Values commonly returned by $procerror following $display Value Error constant Meaning -33 <UGENERR_BATCH_ONLY> Statement not allowed in batch mode. Use a test on $batch to avoid this. -1401 <UPROCERR_PROMPT> Prompted field not valid. Use Allowed in all component types. Related Links $DISPLAY Specify a translation table for display in character-based user interface (CHUI). $keyboard Return or set the current keyboard translation table. Device Translation Tables Device translation tables handle output to screens and printers. They map Uniface logical functions onto device functions, making it possible for Uniface to support a wide variety of hardware platforms, including their output devices. Was this topic helpful?
